question_text: Which of the following best describes the difference between a permanent magnet and an electromagnet?

options.label: A
options.text: Permanent magnets are always stronger than electromagnets

options.label: B
options.text: An electromagnet's magnetism depends on electric current, while a permanent magnet's magnetism is always present

options.label: C
options.text: Only permanent magnets can be turned on and off

options.label: D
options.text: Electromagnets use iron while permanent magnets do not

explanation: The key difference is that electromagnets need electricity to work and can be switched on and off, while permanent magnets are always magnetic without needing a power source.
